chapters.chapter_summary: Introduction of the surgeon and the surgical center. The video begins with the Carolina Surgical Center logo.
Incisions are made at the umbilicus and additional trocars are inserted. The gallbladder is exposed, and dissection begins to identify the cystic duct and artery.
The gallbladder is detached from the liver using an electric cautery device. The cystic duct and artery are clipped and divided. The gallbladder is placed in a bag for removal.
The surgical site is inspected for hemostasis and any bleeding points. The gallbladder is removed and opened to reveal the stones.
The surgical procedure concludes with the Carolina Surgical logo displayed.

SOAP.Subjective: I performed a laparoscopic gallbladder surgery on a patient with suspected gallstones. The patient was calm and cooperative throughout the procedure.
SOAP.Objective: I identified the gallbladder and the surrounding tissues. I observed the gallbladder to be inflamed and enlarged. I performed the dissection to identify the cystic duct and artery, which were clipped and divided. I detached the gallbladder from the liver and placed it in a bag for removal. I inspected the surgical site for hemostasis and found no significant bleeding.
SOAP.Assessment: I assessed the procedure as successful. The gallbladder was removed without complications, and the stones were visible upon opening the gallbladder.
SOAP.Plan: I closed the wound in layers. I will continue to monitor the patient post-operatively and provide necessary pain management and follow-up care.
